questions.question: Given two relations: Employee(EID, Name, DeptID, Salary) and Department(DeptID, DeptName, Location). Write Relational Algebra expressions and corresponding SQL queries for: (a) Find names of employees working in 'Physics' department (b) Find employee IDs and salaries of employees earning more than 50000 (c) Find department IDs that have at least one employee earning above 40000 using NATURAL JOIN.
keyPoints: Include actual table schemas and sample data context
Map each RA operator to equivalent SQL clause

questions.question: Consider relation Enrolled(StudentID, CourseID) and relation Passed(StudentID, CourseID). Using Relational Algebra derived operators, write expressions for: (a) Students who enrolled in at least one course (using UNION) (b) Students who enrolled in all courses (using DIVISION) (c) Students who enrolled in same courses as student ID 'S101' (using intersection).
keyPoints: Apply DIVISION operator for 'all' type queries
Use ∩ (intersection) as derived operator: R ∩ S = R - (R - S)
Include SQL equivalents using IN, EXISTS, and set operators
Show step-by-step decomposition of complex queries

questions.question: Consider a University database: STUDENT(SID, SName, Major, GPA), COURSE(CID, CName, Credits, DeptID), ENROLL(SID, CID, Grade, Semester), INSTRUCTOR(IID, IName, DeptID), TEACHES(IID, CID, Year). Write Relational Algebra expressions using θ-join (⨝), division, and other operators for: (a) Names of students enrolled in all 'Database' courses (b) Instructors who taught every course in 2023 (c) Departments where average GPA of students is above 7.0 (d) Courses taken by students whose GPA > 8.0 in 'Physics' major, showing both RA and SQL.
keyPoints: Use DIVISION for 'enrolled in all' and 'taught every' queries
Apply θ-join (⨝) with conditions for complex filtering
Include grouping and aggregation in SQL mapping
Break down multi-step queries with intermediate relations

questions.question: Given a hospital management system: PATIENT(PatientID, Name, Age, WardNo, AdmissionDate), WARD(WardNo, WardName, Capacity, FloorNo), STAFF(StaffID, Name, Role, WardNo), TREATMENT(PatientID, DoctorID, TreatmentDate, Diagnosis). Write Relational Algebra queries: (a) Find names of patients treated by doctors in 'Cardiology' ward using LEFT OUTER JOIN (b) Find wards with more than 2 staff members using division (c) Find doctors who treated all patients in 'ICU' ward (d) Compare INNER JOIN vs LEFT OUTER JOIN results for patients with their assigned doctors. Include SQL equivalents and explain differences.
keyPoints: Implement OUTER JOIN (⟕, ⟖) as derived operators
Use division to find 'all patients in specific ward' queries
Show how NULL values appear in outer join results
Demonstrate practical scenario with hospital database
